assumptions: npx is the default documented installer transport
scripts/install.sh remains the canonical install engine
the repo stays as a single GitHub repository
the default install path should not create a git checkout for end users
backward compatibility is not a constraint

decisions.decision: Publish the runtime payload as an npm package and consume it from a separate installer package in the same repo
Use @supermemory/install as the zero-knowledge npx entrypoint
Keep curl/bootstrap as a fallback path only

phases.title: Runtime packaging contract
phases.outcome: The runtime can be published and unpacked as a complete install payload

tasks.title: Rename or confirm the publishable runtime package identity
acceptance: A final runtime package name is chosen
The package identity is referenced consistently in docs and release steps

tasks.title: Add a strict runtime package files allowlist
acceptance: npm pack includes scripts/install.sh, docker compose files, runtime source, and required config
Local smoke installs and transient artifacts are excluded

tasks.title: Add tarball content validation
acceptance: A scripted check fails if required runtime files are missing from npm pack output

phases.title: Installer package
phases.outcome: An npx-installable package can materialize the runtime into a final directory and run the canonical installer

tasks.title: Create the installer workspace package
acceptance: The repo contains a publishable @supermemory/install package with an executable bin

tasks.title: Implement CLI argument parsing and mode mapping
acceptance: The installer supports agent, api, and full modes
--mcp <scope> maps cleanly to install.sh flags
A developer-only local source override exists for local testing

tasks.title: Fetch and unpack the runtime into the final install directory
acceptance: The default source is the published runtime tarball
The runtime is unpacked directly into the final directory
The resulting install is not a git checkout by default

tasks.title: Invoke the canonical installer from the final directory
acceptance: The wrapper runs scripts/install.sh from the final directory
Project-scope MCP registration references the final directory

tasks.title: Write an install manifest
acceptance: A machine-readable manifest is written into the installed directory with installer version, runtime version, mode, and timestamp

phases.title: Zero-knowledge output and docs
phases.outcome: The successful install output tells a brand-new user only what to do next

tasks.title: Rewrite mode-aware success output
acceptance: full --mcp project prints cd, Claude, and health-check guidance
agent --mcp project does not tell the user to run npm run mcp after successful registration
Non-applicable skip guidance is omitted

tasks.title: Switch README quick start to npx
acceptance: README quick start begins with the npx installer
curl and manual clone remain only as fallback paths

phases.title: Validation and release
phases.outcome: The npx-first path is proven in clean-room tests and releasable

tasks.title: Add clean-room integration tests
acceptance: Tests cover no-checkout full and agent installs
Tests assert the final .mcp.json path is correct

tasks.title: Add uninstall and purge regression coverage
acceptance: An npx-installed directory can be fully purged and reinstalled

tasks.title: Define publish ordering and smoke-release procedure
acceptance: Release steps publish the runtime before the installer
A smoke test command is defined for the just-published npx installer

success_criteria: A first-time user can install with one npx command and no repo knowledge
The default install path does not leave the user in a git checkout
Project-scope MCP registration points at the final install path
The final output is short, mode-aware, and zero-knowledge friendly
A user can open Claude from the installed directory and persist a memory
